title: Use cobra in skopeo
body:
Use cobra in skopeo can help share code with podman/buildah(code for skopeo login/logout CLI).
https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/839
Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: I’m also somewhat wondering about how the integration into Podman is going to work — the two have different sets of global options, and Podman’s subcommand-independent(?) infrastructure is fairly extensive. But, one step at a time.

> I’m also somewhat wondering about how the integration into Podman is going to work — the two have different sets of global options

As a first guess, the old structure (`func copyCmd(global *globalOptions) Command`) seems broadly acceptable: Podman can have its own parsing of the global options, somehow get them into the `global` structure (that would be new and annoying to maintain, sure), and then invoke `Command.Execute*()` with the remaining options/arguments.

@mtrmac @vrothberg PTAL

override the `IsBoolFlag() bool` seems not work

> override the `IsBoolFlag() bool` seems not work

Could you elaborate, please? I have no idea what is the problem / expected / actual behavior.
Is that about the test failures?

> Is that about the test failures?

Yes.  That's about the test failures. For OptionalBool `--OB true`, the `true` should not be regarded as the value of the --OB.
start #865, keeping optional bool and hiding `cobra.Command` behind the `RunE` seems can not make much code shareable

> @mtrmac PTAL
> I haven't found the method to make OptionalBool work as bool to fix the test.
> Can it be converted to the Flag.BoolVar , like the code in podman or buildah?
>
> ```go
> if c.IsSet("tls-verify") {
> 		dockerRegistryOptions.DockerInsecureSkipTLSVerify = types.NewOptionalBool(!c.TlsVerify)
> 	}
> ```

I don’t think that would actually be good enough: the `TestOptionalBoolIsBoolFlag` flag verifies the behavior of `optionalBool` and the test failing indicates that the value of the `--tls-verify` option would sometimes be treated as `false` when the previous behavior was `true`. That would be a security vulnerability, and a complete blocker to this PR. (_That_, in turn would not quite be a disaster: Podman could call a Skopeo login implementation by filling the `loginOptions` using its own CLI parser. But hopefully we won’t need that.)

Looking at the code, the test can be made to work by using
```go
flag.NoOptDefVal = "true"
```
see (WIP and _very_ unclean) https://github.com/mtrmac/skopeo/tree/corba .

OTOH:
- This _must_ be done in a way that can’t be forgotten by the caller, absolutely not at every call site; i.e. instead of `newOptionalBoolValue`, probably an `optionalBoolFlag(*pflag.FlagSet, …)`.
- Manually setting `NoOptDefVal` is something that can be done in the public API, but it’s pretty ugly and obscure. Still to investigate: using `PFlagFromGoFlag` to let the library do that.

> > @mtrmac PTAL
> > I haven't found the method to make OptionalBool work as bool to fix the test.
> > Can it be converted to the Flag.BoolVar , like the code in podman or buildah?
> > ```go
> > if c.IsSet("tls-verify") {
> > 		dockerRegistryOptions.DockerInsecureSkipTLSVerify = types.NewOptionalBool(!c.TlsVerify)
> > 	}
> > ```
> 
> I don’t think that would actually be good enough

… thinking about it more, `flag.BoolVar` does set `NoOptDefVal = "true"` internally, so that could actually work. Still, `"tls-verify"` is _exactly_ the reason the string lookups in command implementations are unwanted (https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/523 ).
@mtrmac PTAL. Use `optionalBoolFlag(*pflag.flagSet..)`. works for tests
urfave/cli flag `--generate-bash-completion` used in `completions/bash/skopeo` would not work. Does the whole completions/bash/skopeo need rewrite?

> Does the whole completions/bash/skopeo need rewrite?

Probably — OTOH Cobra has its own completion generation support, and it seems to be fairly rich in features. So the shell script might end up being much simpler.
@mtrmac PTAL
@mtrmac PTAL. can this PR get merged?
@mtrmac @vrothberg PTAL
(I apologize for the delay; I’ll review this today unless something extremely urgent comes up.)
@mtrmac PTAL
@mtrmac I cleaned the reviews. PTAL
@mtrmac PTAL
PTAL. Can this get merged or more fixes needed?
